concept_ref: §3 row 1 — '100% statement coverage = fully tested' / §4 Level 1 narrative

scenario.title: The Missing Else
scenario.narrative: A pricing function applies a discount only to premium users. The 'happy path' test was added; the team claims testing is complete.

claim: Does the team's claim — 'T1 achieves 100% Statement coverage, therefore the function is fully tested' — hold up?
charges: The pricing function price(amount, is_premium) applies a discount only when is_premium is true and contains no else branch.
The defendant team submitted a single happy-path test (is_premium=True) and claims the function is fully tested.
The prosecution alleges the implicit FALSE edge of the if statement is never exercised, leaving non-premium pricing logic unverified.

options.label: Yes — every line ran, so the function is fully tested.
options.explanation: Trap. Statement coverage IS 100% — but 'fully tested' also requires Branch coverage. The implicit FALSE edge of the if (is_premium=False) is NEVER taken, so an else-path bug stays hidden.

options.label: No — Statement is 100%, but the FALSE branch of the if is untested.
options.explanation: Correct. T1 only exercises is_premium=True. Statement coverage is satisfied (every line runs), but Branch coverage is 50% — the implicit else-path is never taken. 'Fully tested' is a misconception.

correct_answer_explanation: Statement is 100% but Branch is 50%. A non-premium user could be mishandled and Statement testing would never catch it.
wrong_answer_explanation: An if-without-else creates a hidden branch that Statement coverage cannot see — you also need a test where is_premium is FALSE.
hints: The first half of the claim is true; the second half is the trap. Statement coverage IS 100%, but does that mean the function is fully tested?
Statement coverage only asks 'did each line execute?' — but what about the implicit FALSE branch of the if? Is there a test where is_premium=False?
Statement coverage IS 100% (every line executed via T1), but Branch coverage is 0% on the false edge. 'Fully tested' is a misconception. Verdict: claim INVALID.

seeded_faults.description: Hidden bug on the implicit else path: a non-premium user is double-charged due to a missing reset.

misconceptions.explanation_md: 100% Statement coverage does NOT mean 'fully tested'. An `if` without an `else` creates a bypass path that Statement coverage cannot see. You must add a test where the condition is FALSE to exercise the implicit branch.
